evals.expected_output: A numbered list of books from the Want to Read shelf
expectations: Claude invokes `npx tsx src/cli.ts list` (or equivalent npm script)
The response includes a numbered or bulleted list of book titles
The response mentions how many books are shown

evals.prompt: i just started reading project hail mary by andy weir
evals.expected_output: Confirms the book is marked as Currently Reading
expectations: Claude invokes `npx tsx src/cli.ts start --title` with a title matching 'Project Hail Mary'
The response confirms the book was marked as Currently Reading
Claude echoes back the resolved title after the mutation succeeds

evals.expected_output: Confirms the book is marked as Read with a date
expectations: Claude invokes `npx tsx src/cli.ts finish --title` with a title matching 'Dune'
The response confirms the book was marked as Read
If the title resolves ambiguously (multiple editions), Claude asks the user to choose before mutating

evals.prompt: how many books did i read last year?
evals.expected_output: Reports a count of books read in the previous calendar year
expectations: The response includes a numeric count of books
The response mentions which year the count is for

evals.prompt: add the three body problem to my to read shelf
evals.expected_output: Claude recognizes this as a Want to Read intent and explains current limitations or attempts to handle it
expectations: Claude recognizes the user wants to add a book to their Want to Read shelf
Claude either attempts to fulfill the request or clearly explains that the current skill only supports list/start/finish/count operations
Claude does not silently ignore the request

evals.prompt: show me the last 5 books i've been reading
evals.expected_output: Lists books from the Currently Reading shelf
expectations: Claude invokes a list or shelf command to fetch currently-reading books
The response shows book titles the user is currently reading
Claude limits results appropriately based on the user's request
